subject: English / First Flight
chapter_name: Poems – How to Tell Wild Animals, The Ball Poem
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the main events and ideas of both poems
Identify humour, exaggeration, and observation in 'How to Tell Wild Animals'
Recognize themes of loss and responsibility in 'The Ball Poem'
Develop basic comprehension and summarization skills
Encourage creative expression through writing and drawing

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poems and Poets
topics_covered: Authors: Carolyn Wells and John Berryman
Simple introduction to humour, observation, and loss
teaching_strategy: Introduce each poet and explain their style simply
Ask students to share experiences of noticing animals or losing something
student_activities: Discuss the titles and predict the content of each poem
Share personal experiences of observation or loss
assessment: Oral Q&A on poets and poem titles

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehension – How to Tell Wild Animals
topics_covered: Explain difficult words: tawny, advance, discern
Understand how animals are described humorously
teaching_strategy: Read line by line and explain in simple terms
Encourage visualization of each animal described
assessment: Ask students to identify one animal and describe its traits

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehension – The Ball Poem
topics_covered: Discuss simple meaning: loss, responsibility, and reflection
Understand the boy’s feelings and life lesson
teaching_strategy: Explain lines simply and relate to students’ experiences
Highlight the boy’s emotions and learning process
student_activities: Identify words showing grief or realization
assessment: Oral Q&A on the boy’s feelings

lesson_plan.title: Themes, Humour, and Reflection
topics_covered: Compare the two poems: humour vs reflection
Discuss symbols and playful exaggeration in animal descriptions
Understand lessons from loss and responsibility
teaching_strategy: Create a simple chart showing humour in animal descriptions vs reflection on loss
Use examples from poems to explain key points
student_activities: Make a chart of funny animal descriptions vs emotional reflections
Discuss how poems teach different lessons
assessment: Oral Q&A on themes and humour

lesson_plan.title: Creative Expression and Reflection
topics_covered: Expressing thoughts through writing or drawing
Reflecting on humour, observation, loss, and responsibility
teaching_strategy: Encourage students to connect poems to their experiences
Summarize key points on the board
student_activities: Write a short paragraph imagining identifying a wild animal or losing something valuable
Draw an illustration of an animal from the poem or the boy’s ball floating away
assessment: Class discussion on lessons learned from poems

teaching_aids: Printed or projected texts of both poems
Images of wild animals for visualization
Charts for humour, observation, and reflection
Flashcards with keywords and difficult words
teacher_tip: Keep explanations simple and interactive. Emphasize the humour in animal descriptions and the emotional depth in 'The Ball Poem'. Encourage students to relate poems to their own experiences.
